special_mechanics.name: Ring Exit
special_mechanics.description: Players may leave the ring but must re-enter before a 20-count by the referee. Failure results in count-out loss or double count-out draw. Referee count continues as long as at least one wrestler remains outside.

special_mechanics.name: Turnbuckle Climbing
special_mechanics.description: Wrestlers can climb the top two turnbuckles to execute high-flying attacks

special_mechanics.name: Lock-Up System
special_mechanics.description: Wrestlers can lock up with each other to execute body slams, piledrivers, and other professional wrestling moves

special_mechanics.name: Pin Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Downed opponents can be pinned for a three-count. Referee must run to pin location and lie down to count, creating positional advantage/disadvantage based on referee distance

tier_notes: Tier 2: This is a beat-em-up/wrestling game where available documentation focuses on game rules and design rather than precise physics parameters. Movement and gravity values are not quantified in sources. Assembly code snippets from TCRF show animation/sprite offset data but not physics constants.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor (tcrf.net) - Pro Wrestling NES article with assembly code and regional differences
Wikipedia - Pro Wrestling (NES video game) - Gameplay mechanics and development information
